   493→/-!
   494→### The w-contractification of a w-strictly-local ring
   495→
   496→The main result `exists_isWContractibleRing_of_isWStrictlyLocal` constructs an ind-Zariski,
   497→faithfully flat, w-contractible cover of any w-strictly-local ring. The proof follows the
   498→blueprint (thm:ind-etale-w-contractible-cover-of-w-strictly-local, Stacks 0983):
   499→
   500→1. By Gleason's theorem (`StoneCech.projective` + `CompactT2.Projective.extremallyDisconnected`),
   501→   choose an extremally disconnected profinite space `T` (= `Ultrafilter (pi_0(Spec R))`)
   502→   with a surjection `T -> pi_0(Spec R)`.
   503→2. By the Pullback construction (`WContractification.Pullback`), taking the colimit over
   504→   all discrete quotients of `T`, we get a ring `D` that is ind-Zariski and faithfully flat
   505→   over `R`, with `pi_0(Spec D) = T` (hence extremally disconnected).
   506→3. The local rings of `D` at maximal ideals are isomorphic to the corresponding local rings
   507→   of `R` (by `Algebra.IndZariski.bijectiveOnStalks_algebraMap`), hence strictly Henselian.
   508→4. Therefore `D` is w-contractible.
   509→
   510→The construction of the "profinite Pullback" (colimit of `WContractification.Pullback S f`
   511→over `S : DiscreteQuotient T`) and verification of its properties (`pi_0(Spec D) = T`,
   512→cartesian diagram, w-local structure, faithfully flat) requires substantial infrastructure
   513→that is stated as an admitted helper lemma below.
   514→-/
   515→
   516→-- Helper: the existence of a w-contractible cover, with the detailed construction admitted.
   517→-- This corresponds to `thm:ind-etale-w-contractible-cover-of-w-strictly-local` in the blueprint
   518→-- and `Stacks 0983` (second half).
   519→-- The construction uses:
   520→-- (a) Gleason's theorem: Ultrafilter(pi_0(Spec A)) is extremally disconnected
   521→--     (Mathlib: StoneCech.projective + CompactT2.Projective.extremallyDisconnected)
   522→-- (b) The profinite Pullback: colimit of WContractification.Pullback S f over
   523→--     S : DiscreteQuotient (Ultrafilter (pi_0(Spec A))), cf. def:modify-pi0-profinite
   524→-- (c) Properties of the profinite Pullback (Stacks 097D):
   525→--     * ind-Zariski over A (colimit of ind-Zariski is ind-Zariski)
   526→--     * pi_0(Spec D) = T (from the cartesian diagram, Stacks 096C)
   527→--     * D is w-local (from WLocal/Pullback.lean, fully proved)
   528→--     * D is faithfully flat (from Module.Flat.of_indZariski + surjectivity of Spec.comap)
   529→-- (d) Stalks at maximal ideals are strictly Henselian:
   530→--     by bijectiveOnStalks_algebraMap (fully proved in IndZariski.lean) +
   531→--     transfer of strictly Henselian property through ring isomorphism
   532→-- Individual pieces (a), (c-w-local part), (d-bijectiveOnStalks) are fully proved.
   533→-- Missing infrastructure: (b) profinite Pullback definition + (c-remaining) its properties.
   534→private lemma exists_wContractibleCover (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsWStrictlyLocalRing A] :
   535→    ∃ (D : Type u) (_ : CommRing D) (_ : Algebra A D),
   536→      Algebra.IndZariski A D ∧ Module.FaithfullyFlat A D ∧ IsWContractibleRing D := by
   537→  -- Blueprint: thm:ind-etale-w-contractible-cover-of-w-strictly-local (Stacks 0983).
   538→  sorry
   539→
   540→/-- Any w-strictly-local ring has an ind-Zariski, faithfully flat cover that is w-contractible. -/
   541→lemma exists_isWContractibleRing_of_isWStrictlyLocal
   542→    [IsWStrictlyLocalRing R] :
   543→    ∃ (S : Type u) (_ : CommRing S) (_ : Algebra R S),
   544→      Algebra.IndZariski R S ∧ Module.FaithfullyFlat R S ∧ IsWContractibleRing S :=
   545→  exists_wContractibleCover R
   546→
   547→/-- Any ring has an ind-étale, faithfully flat cover that is w-contractible. -/
   548→theorem exists_isWContractibleRing :
   549→    ∃ (S : Type u) (_ : CommRing S) (_ : Algebra R S),
   550→      Algebra.IndEtale R S ∧ Module.FaithfullyFlat R S ∧ IsWContractibleRing S := by
   551→  obtain ⟨S, _, _, _, _, _⟩ :=
   552→    exists_isWContractibleRing_of_isWStrictlyLocal (WStrictLocalization R)
   553→  letI : Algebra R S := Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap R (WStrictLocalization R))
   554→  have : IsScalarTower R (WStrictLocalization R) S := .of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
   555→  refine ⟨S, inferInstance, inferInstance, ?_, ?_, inferInstance⟩
   556→  · exact Algebra.IndEtale.trans _ (WStrictLocalization R) _
   557→  · exact Module.FaithfullyFlat.trans _ (WStrictLocalization R) _
   558→
   559→/-- Any ring has an ind-étale, faithfully flat cover for which every ind-étale
   560→faithfully flat cover splits. -/
   561→theorem exists_forall_exists_retraction :
   562→    ∃ (S : Type u) (_ : CommRing S) (_ : Algebra R S),
   563→      Algebra.IndEtale R S ∧ Module.FaithfullyFlat R S ∧
   564→      ∀ (T : Type u) [CommRing T] [Algebra S T] [Algebra.IndEtale S T] [Module.FaithfullyFlat S T],
   565→        ∃ (f : T →+* S), f.comp (algebraMap S T) = RingHom.id S := by
   566→  obtain ⟨S, _, _, _, _, _⟩ := exists_isWContractibleRing R
   567→  use S, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance
   568→  intro T _ _ _ _
   569→  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := IsWContractibleRing.exists_retraction S T
   570→  use f, hf

private lemma exists_retraction_of_bijectiveOnStalks [IsWLocalRing R]
    (hED : ExtremallyDisconnected (ConnectedComponents (PrimeSpectrum R)))
    {I : Ideal R} (hI : zeroLocus I = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R))
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [Module.FaithfullyFlat R S] [IsWLocalRing S]
    (hS : zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)) = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum S))
    (hbij : (algebraMap R S).BijectiveOnStalks) :
    ∃ (f : S →+* R), f.comp (algebraMap R S) = RingHom.id R := by
  -- The closed points V(IS) map to V(I) via Spec(algebraMap R S).
  -- V(I) ≅ π₀(Spec R) is extremally disconnected (compact T2 projective).
  -- So the surjection π₀(Spec S) → π₀(Spec R) has a section, giving a retraction
  -- of S via the Restriction construction and the isomorphism theorem.
  -- The full proof requires:
  -- 1. Show V(IS) → V(I) is surjective (from faithfully flat)
  -- 2. Get section using extremally disconnected projectivity
  -- 3. Use Restriction construction to get S → S_T
  -- 4. Show R ≅ S_T using RingHom.IsWLocal.bijective_of_bijective
  -- 5. Compose to get retraction
  -- All steps except (4) use formalized infrastructure.
  -- Step (4) depends on the sorry'd RingHom.IsWLocal.bijective_of_bijective.
  -- Blueprint: thm:ff-identifies-local-rings-plus-c-has-retraction-if. Blocked on RingHom.IsWLocal.bijective_of_bijective.
  sorry
input.new_string:
private lemma exists_retraction_of_bijectiveOnStalks [IsWLocalRing R]
    (hED : ExtremallyDisconnected (ConnectedComponents (PrimeSpectrum R)))
    {I : Ideal R} (hI : zeroLocus I = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R))
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [Module.FaithfullyFlat R S] [IsWLocalRing S]
    (hS : zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)) = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum S))
    (hbij : (algebraMap R S).BijectiveOnStalks) :
    ∃ (f : S →+* R), f.comp (algebraMap R S) = RingHom.id R := by
  -- Step 1: The map on closed points V(IS) → V(I) is surjective (from faithfully flat)
  have hsurf : Function.Surjective (Set.MapsTo.restrict
      (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S))
      (zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)))
      (zeroLocus I)
      (fun p hp => PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp)) := by
    intro ⟨p, hp⟩
    obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := PrimeSpectrum.specComap_surjective_of_faithfullyFlat (A := R) (B := S) p
    exact ⟨⟨q, hq ▸ PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp⟩, Subtype.ext hq⟩
  sorry

Let me read the CompactT2.Projective definition to understand how to use it:

75→/-- The assertion `CompactT2.Projective` states that given continuous maps
    76→`f : X → Z` and `g : Y → Z` with `g` surjective between `t_2`, compact topological spaces,
    77→there exists a continuous lift `h : X → Y`, such that `f = g ∘ h`. -/
    78→def CompactT2.Projective : Prop :=
    79→  ∀ {Y Z : Type u} [TopologicalSpace Y] [TopologicalSpace Z],
    80→    ∀ [CompactSpace Y] [T2Space Y] [CompactSpace Z] [T2Space Z],
    81→      ∀ {f : X → Z} {g : Y → Z} (_ : Continuous f) (_ : Continuous g) (_ : Surjective g),
    82→        ∃ h : X → Y, Continuous h ∧ g ∘ h = f
    83→
    84→variable {X}
    85→
    86→theorem StoneCech.projective [DiscreteTopology X] : CompactT2.Projective (StoneCech X) := by
    87→  intro Y Z _tsY _tsZ _csY _t2Y _csZ _csZ f g hf hg g_sur
    88→  let s : Z → Y := fun z => Classical.choose <| g_sur z
    89→  have hs : g ∘ s = id := funext fun z => Classical.choose_spec (g_sur z)
    90→  let t := s ∘ f ∘ stoneCechUnit
    91→  have ht : Continuous t := continuous_of_discreteTopology
    92→  let h : StoneCech X → Y := stoneCechExtend ht
    93→  have hh : Continuous h := continuous_stoneCechExtend ht
    94→  refine ⟨h, hh, denseRange_stoneCechUnit.equalizer (hg.comp hh) hf ?_⟩
    95→  rw [comp_assoc, stoneCechExtend_extends ht, ← comp_assoc, hs, id_comp]
    96→
    97→protected theorem CompactT2.Projective.extremallyDisconnected [CompactSpace X] [T2Space X]
    98→    (h : CompactT2.Projective X) : ExtremallyDisconnected X := by
    99→  refine { open_closure := fun U hU => ?_ }
   100→  let Z₁ : Set (X × Bool) := Uᶜ ×ˢ {true}
   101→  let Z₂ : Set (X × Bool) := closure U ×ˢ {false}
   102→  let Z : Set (X × Bool) := Z₁ ∪ Z₂
   103→  have hZ₁₂ : Disjoint Z₁ Z₂ := disjoint_left.2 fun x hx₁ hx₂ => by cases hx₁.2.symm.trans hx₂.2
   104→  have hZ₁ : IsClosed Z₁ := hU.isClosed_compl.prod (T1Space.t1 _)

private lemma exists_retraction_of_bijectiveOnStalks [IsWLocalRing R]
    (hED : ExtremallyDisconnected (ConnectedComponents (PrimeSpectrum R)))
    {I : Ideal R} (hI : zeroLocus I = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R))
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [Module.FaithfullyFlat R S] [IsWLocalRing S]
    (hS : zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)) = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum S))
    (hbij : (algebraMap R S).BijectiveOnStalks) :
    ∃ (f : S →+* R), f.comp (algebraMap R S) = RingHom.id R := by
  -- Step 1: The map on closed points V(IS) → V(I) is surjective (from faithfully flat)
  have hsurf : Function.Surjective (Set.MapsTo.restrict
      (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S))
      (zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)))
      (zeroLocus I)
      (fun p hp => PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp)) := by
    intro ⟨p, hp⟩
    obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := PrimeSpectrum.specComap_surjective_of_faithfullyFlat (A := R) (B := S) p
    exact ⟨⟨q, hq ▸ PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp⟩, Subtype.ext hq⟩
  sorry

private lemma exists_retraction_of_bijectiveOnStalks [IsWLocalRing R]
    (hED : ExtremallyDisconnected (ConnectedComponents (PrimeSpectrum R)))
    {I : Ideal R} (hI : zeroLocus I = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R))
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [Module.FaithfullyFlat R S] [IsWLocalRing S]
    (hS : zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)) = closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum S))
    (hbij : (algebraMap R S).BijectiveOnStalks) :
    ∃ (f : S →+* R), f.comp (algebraMap R S) = RingHom.id R := by
  -- Step 1: Comap on closed points is surjective (from faithfully flat)
  have hsurf_comap : Function.Surjective (Set.MapsTo.restrict
      (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S))
      (zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)))
      (zeroLocus I)
      (fun p hp => PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp)) := by
    intro ⟨p, hp⟩
    obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := PrimeSpectrum.specComap_surjective_of_faithfullyFlat (A := R) (B := S) p
    exact ⟨⟨q, hq ▸ PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp⟩, Subtype.ext hq⟩
  -- Step 2: Use homeomorphisms to get surjection on connected components
  haveI : WLocalSpace (PrimeSpectrum R) := IsWLocalRing.wLocalSpace_primeSepectrum
  haveI : WLocalSpace (PrimeSpectrum S) := IsWLocalRing.wLocalSpace_primeSepectrum
  let φR := WLocalSpace.closedPointsHomeomorph (X := PrimeSpectrum R)
  let φS := WLocalSpace.closedPointsHomeomorph (X := PrimeSpectrum S)
  have hsurf_cc : Function.Surjective ((φR.symm ∘ Set.MapsTo.restrict
      (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S))
      (zeroLocus (I.map (algebraMap R S)))
      (zeroLocus I)
      (fun p hp => PrimeSpectrum.comap_zeroLocus_le (algebraMap R S) I hp)) ∘ φS) := by
    rw [hI, hS] at hsurf_comap
    exact Function.Surjective.comp (Function.Surjective.comp hsurf_comap φR.symm.surjective) φS.surjective
  sorry

1→# Archon Project
     2→
     3→You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
     4→
     5→## Priority Rule
     6→
     7→If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
     8→- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
     9→- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
    10→- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
    11→
    12→When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
    13→
    14→## Skills
    15→- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
    16→
    17→## Tools
    18→- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
    19→- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
    20→
    21→## Key Files & Permissions
    22→
    23→All state files are in `.archon/`:
    24→
    25→| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Review Agent | User |
    26→|------|-----------|-------------|-------------|------|
    27→| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    28→| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | write |
    29→| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    30→| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    31→| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | read only | read |
    32→| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    33→| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    34→| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only) | do not edit | write (via comments) |
    35→
    36→## User Interaction
    37→
    38→Users provide hints in two places:
    39→
    40→- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
    41→- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
    42→
    43→## Agent Roles
    44→
    45→### Plan Agent
    46→- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
    47→- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
    48→- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
    49→- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
    50→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
    51→
    52→### Prover Agent
    53→- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
    54→- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
    55→  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
    56→  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
    57→  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
    58→- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
    59→- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
    60→- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
    61→
    62→### Review Agent
    63→- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
    64→- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
    65→- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
    66→- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
    67→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1→# Prover — Prover Stage
     2→
     3→You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
     4→
     5→## Workflow
     6→
     7→1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
     8→2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
     9→3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
    10→4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
    11→5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
    12→6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
    13→7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
    14→
    15→**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
    16→
    17→## Avoid Early Termination
    18→
    19→- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
    20→- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
    21→- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
    22→- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
    23→- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
    24→- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
    25→
    26→## Task Completion Criteria
    27→
    28→Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
    29→1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
    30→2. Zero axioms introduced
    31→3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
    32→
    33→If you encounter obstacles:
    34→- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
    35→- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
    36→- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
    37→- Try alternative proof strategies
    38→- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
    39→- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
    40→
    41→### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
    42→
    43→Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
    44→
    45→1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
    46→2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
    47→3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
    48→   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
    49→   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
    50→   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
    51→
    52→## Proof Style
    53→
    54→- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
    55→- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
    56→- Do not add unrelated declarations
    57→- **Initial definitions and final theorem/lemma statements are frozen** — do not modify them. If a statement appears wrong, keep the file compilable (use scoped `sorry`), explain why in `task_pending.md`, and let the plan agent decide.
    58→- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
    59→- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
    60→
    61→## Search Protocol
    62→
    63→Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
    64→
    65→1. `lean_local_search` first
    66→2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
    67→3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
    68→4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
    69→
    70→## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
    71→
    72→Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
    73→- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
    74→- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
    75→
    76→## Logging
    77→
    78→Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).

    80→**Format:**
    81→
    82→```markdown
    83→# Algebra/WLocal.lean

    87→- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
    88→- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available

    91→### Attempt 2

    93→- **Result:** RESOLVED
    94→- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
    95→
    96→## helper_bijective (line 78)
    97→### Attempt 1

    99→- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
   100→- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
   101→- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
   102→```
   103→
   104→**Rules:**
   105→1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
   106→2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
   107→3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
   108→4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
   109→
   110→**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.

   112→## Summary Pipeline
   113→
   114→1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
   115→2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
   116→3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file

1→import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.GoingDown
     2→import Mathlib.Topology.JacobsonSpace
     3→
     4→-- after `Algebra.HasGoingDown.iff_generalizingMap_primeSpectrumComap`
     5→theorem Algebra.HasGoingDown.specComap_surjective_of_closedPoints_subset_preimage
     6→    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [Algebra.HasGoingDown R S]
     7→    (h : closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R) ⊆ Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S))) :
     8→    Function.Surjective (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) := by
     9→  rintro ⟨p, hp⟩
    10→  obtain ⟨m, hm, hle⟩  := Ideal.exists_le_maximal _ hp.ne_top
    11→  have : ⟨m, hm.isPrime⟩ ∈ closedPoints (PrimeSpectrum R) := by
    12→    rwa [mem_closedPoints_iff, PrimeSpectrum.isClosed_singleton_iff_isMaximal]
    13→  obtain ⟨⟨n, _⟩, hn⟩ := h this
    14→  have : n.LiesOver m := ⟨PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff.mp hn.symm⟩
    15→  obtain ⟨q, _, hq, hpq⟩ := Ideal.exists_ideal_le_liesOver_of_le n hle
    16→  use ⟨q, hq⟩, PrimeSpectrum.ext hpq.over.symm
    17→
    18→theorem Algebra.HasGoingDown.localization_bijective_of_subsingleton {R S : Type*}
    19→    [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    20→    [Algebra.HasGoingDown R S] (p : Ideal R) (q : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] [q.IsPrime]
    21→    [q.LiesOver p]
    22→    (h : ∀ (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime], Subsingleton {q : Ideal S // q.IsPrime ∧ q.LiesOver p}) :
    23→    IsLocalization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl) (Localization.AtPrime q) := by
    24→  -- Stacks 00EA: Under uniqueness, S_q = S_p (localization at q equals localization at image of p)
    25→  -- Key: algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl ≤ q.primeCompl
    26→  -- The uniqueness hypothesis h implies the two localizations coincide
    27→  -- Proof strategy: Use isLocalization_iff characterization
    28→  rw [isLocalization_iff]
    29→  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    30→  · -- Elements of algebraMapSubmonoid S p.primeCompl become units
    31→    intro ⟨s, r, hr, hs⟩
    32→    have : s ∈ q.primeCompl := by
    33→      simp only [Ideal.mem_primeCompl_iff] at hr ⊢
    34→      intro hq
    35→      rw [← hs] at hq
    36→      rw [← Ideal.mem_of_liesOver q p r] at hq
    37→      exact hr hq
    38→    exact IsLocalization.map_units _ ⟨s, this⟩
    39→  · -- Every element is a fraction - but this is the hard part
    40→    -- Need to show elements can be expressed using the smaller submonoid
    41→    sorry
    42→  · -- Injectivity condition - also requires the uniqueness hypothesis
    43→    sorry
    44→

content:
# Lemma 10.41.11: Localization and Going Down

**Full Statement:**
Given a ring map $R \to S$ and a prime $\mathfrak{p} \subset R$, if there's a unique prime $\mathfrak{q} \subset S$ lying over $\mathfrak{p}$, and either going up holds or (going down holds with at most one prime of $S$ above each prime of $R$), then $S_{\mathfrak{p}} = S_{\mathfrak{q}}$.

**Proof Sketch:**

The proof shows that any prime $\mathfrak{q}' \subset S$ corresponding to a point in $\text{Spec}(S_{\mathfrak{p}})$ must actually lie in $\text{Spec}(S_{\mathfrak{q}})$, making the map bijective.

Under **going up**: Given $\mathfrak{q}'$ with $\mathfrak{p}' = R \cap \mathfrak{q}' \subseteq \mathfrak{p}$, going up produces a prime $\mathfrak{q}'' \supseteq \mathfrak{q}'$ lying over $\mathfrak{p}$. By uniqueness, $\mathfrak{q}'' = \mathfrak{q}$, so $\mathfrak{q}'$ is a point of $\text{Spec}(S_{\mathfrak{q}})$.

Under **going down**: Going down yields a prime $\mathfrak{q}'' \subseteq \mathfrak{q}$ lying over $\mathfrak{p}'$. By uniqueness of primes over $\mathfrak{p}'$, we get $\mathfrak{q}' = \mathfrak{q}''$, again placing $\mathfrak{q}'$ in $\text{Spec}(S_{\mathfrak{q}})$.

The bijectivity implies all elements of $S - \mathfrak{q}$ become invertible in $S_{\mathfrak{p}}$, yielding the equality.

    [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    [Algebra.HasGoingDown R S] (p : Ideal R) (q : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] [q.IsPrime]

The **Going-Down Theorem** is a fundamental result in commutative algebra concerning the lifting of prime ideal chains in integral extensions. When proving it, localization is a standard and powerful technique, often used to reduce the problem to a local setting where prime ideals are easier to manipulate.

### The Going-Down Theorem
Let $A \subseteq B$ be an integral extension of domains where $A$ is a **normal** (integrally closed) domain. Let $\mathfrak{p}_1 \subset \mathfrak{p}_2$ be a chain of prime ideals in $A$, and let $\mathfrak{q}_2$ be a prime ideal in $B$ lying over $\mathfrak{p}_2$ (i.e., $\mathfrak{q}_2 \cap A = \mathfrak{p}_2$). Then there exists a prime ideal $\mathfrak{q}_1 \subset \mathfrak{q}_2$ such that $\mathfrak{q}_1 \cap A = \mathfrak{p}_1$.

### The Proof Technique: Localization
The proof typically proceeds by moving from the rings $A$ and $B$ to their localizations, which simplifies the structure of the primes involved:

1.  **Reduce to the Local Case:** Let $S = A \setminus \mathfrak{p}_1$. Consider the localized extension $S^{-1}A \subseteq S^{-1}B$. By passing to this localization, the prime $\mathfrak{p}_1$ becomes the unique maximal ideal of the local ring $S^{-1}A$.
2.  **Constructing the Prime:** To find $\mathfrak{q}_1$, one considers the ideal generated by $\mathfrak{p}_1$ in the localization, often denoted $\mathfrak{p}_1 B_{\mathfrak{q}_2}$ (or similar, depending on the specific setup). The goal is to show that this ideal does not "climb up" to intersect $S$ (which would mean the ideal is the unit ideal), thereby allowing the use of Zorn's Lemma or the existence of prime ideals in the localized ring.
3.  **The Role of Normality:** The hypothesis that $A$ is **integrally closed** is crucial here. It ensures that the intersection of the extension $\mathfrak{p}_1 B_{\mathfrak{q}_2}$ back with $A$ is exactly $\mathfrak{p}_1$. Without this, the localization might collapse, preventing the construction of the required prime ideal $\mathfrak{q}_1$.

### Uniqueness and Lying Over
The Going-Down Theorem does **not** inherently claim the *uniqueness* of the prime lying over $\mathfrak{p}_1$. 
*   **Lying-Over:** Ensures at least one such prime exists.
*   **Incomparability:** States that if you have two primes in $B$ lying over the same prime in $A$, neither contains the other.
*   **Uniqueness:** If a prime $\mathfrak{q}$ in $B$ lying over $\mathfrak{p}$ is unique, it often implies specific conditions on the localizations (e.g., $B_{\mathfrak{p}} = B_{\mathfrak{q}}$). 

In many advanced contexts (like in the theory of schemes or Dedekind domains), if a prime lying over is unique, it simplifies the transition between the spectra of the rings. If you are looking for uniqueness, you are likely looking at cases where the extension is particularly well-behaved, such as in **flat extensions** or when dealing with specific types of algebraic number fields where the prime splitting behavior is restricted.

    44→
    45→### Plan Agent
    46→- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
    47→- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
    48→- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
    49→- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
    50→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
    51→
    52→### Prover Agent
    53→- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
    54→- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
    55→  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
    56→  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
    57→  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
    58→- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
    59→- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
    60→- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
    61→
    62→### Review Agent
    63→- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
    64→- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
    65→- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
    66→- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
    67→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
    68→

1→# Prover — Prover Stage
     2→
     3→You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
     4→
     5→## Workflow
     6→
     7→1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
     8→2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
     9→3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
    10→4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
    11→5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
    12→6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
    13→7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
    14→
    15→**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
    16→
    17→## Avoid Early Termination
    18→
    19→- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
    20→- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
    21→- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
    22→- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
    23→- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
    24→- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
    25→
    26→## Task Completion Criteria
    27→
    28→Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
    29→1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
    30→2. Zero axioms introduced
    31→3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
    32→
    33→If you encounter obstacles:
    34→- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
    35→- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
    36→- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
    37→- Try alternative proof strategies
    38→- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
    39→- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
    40→
    41→### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
    42→
    43→Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
    44→
    45→1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
    46→2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
    47→3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
    48→   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
    49→   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
    50→   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
    51→
    52→## Proof Style
    53→
    54→- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
    55→- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
    56→- Do not add unrelated declarations
    57→- **Initial definitions and final theorem/lemma statements are frozen** — do not modify them. If a statement appears wrong, keep the file compilable (use scoped `sorry`), explain why in `task_pending.md`, and let the plan agent decide.
    58→- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
    59→- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
    60→
    61→## Search Protocol
    62→
    63→Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
    64→
    65→1. `lean_local_search` first
    66→2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
    67→3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
    68→4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
    69→
    70→## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
    71→
    72→Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
    73→- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
    74→- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
    75→
    76→## Logging
    77→
    78→Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
    79→
    80→**Format:**
    81→
    82→```markdown
    83→# Algebra/WLocal.lean
    84→
    85→## wLocal_iff (line 45)
    86→### Attempt 1
    87→- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
    88→- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
    89→- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
    90→
    91→### Attempt 2
    92→- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
    93→- **Result:** RESOLVED
    94→- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
    95→
    96→## helper_bijective (line 78)
    97→### Attempt 1
    98→- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
    99→- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
   100→- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
   101→- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
   102→```
   103→
   104→**Rules:**
   105→1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
   106→2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
   107→3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
   108→4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
   109→
   110→**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
   111→
   112→## Summary Pipeline
   113→
   114→1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
   115→2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
   116→3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
   117→4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
   118→5. Do not modify initial definitions or final theorem/lemma statements. Only fill in proof bodies and add helper lemmas. Intermediate helpers you introduced may be corrected.
   119→6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
   120→7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
   121→8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
   122→
   123→## End-of-session handoff
   124→
   125→Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
   126→
   127→1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
   128→   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
   129→   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
   130→   - Concrete next step for the next session
   131→   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
   132→2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
   133→

    15→/-!
    16→# Affine étale site
    17→-/
    18→
    19→universe u
    20→
    21→open CategoryTheory Opposite Limits
    22→
    23→namespace CategoryTheory
    24→
    25→section
    26→
    27→variable {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] (F : C ⥤ D) (X : D)
    28→
    29→instance (J : Type*) [Category J] [IsConnected J] [HasLimitsOfShape J C]
    30→    [PreservesLimitsOfShape J F]
    31→    [HasLimitsOfShape J D] :
    32→    PreservesLimitsOfShape J (CostructuredArrow.toOver F X) where
    33→  preservesLimit {K} := by
    34→    have : PreservesLimitsOfShape J (CostructuredArrow.proj F X) := by
    35→      infer_instance
    36→    have : PreservesLimit K (CostructuredArrow.toOver F X ⋙ Over.forget X) := by
    37→      show PreservesLimit K (CostructuredArrow.proj F X ⋙ F)
    38→      infer_instance
    39→    have : HasLimit (K ⋙ CostructuredArrow.toOver F X) := by
    40→      infer_instance
    41→    have : PreservesLimit (K ⋙ CostructuredArrow.toOver F X) (Over.forget X) := by
    42→      infer_instance
    43→    have : ReflectsLimit (K ⋙ CostructuredArrow.toOver F X) (Over.forget X) :=
    44→      reflectsLimit_of_reflectsIsomorphisms _ _
    45→    apply Limits.preservesLimit_of_reflects_of_preserves _ (Over.forget X)
    46→
    47→end
    48→
    49→namespace MorphismProperty
    50→
    51→variable {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D]
    52→variable (P : MorphismProperty D) (Q : MorphismProperty C) [Q.IsMultiplicative] (F : C ⥤ D) (X : D)
    53→
    54→namespace CostructuredArrow
    55→
    56→variable [P.IsStableUnderBaseChange]
    57→  [P.IsStableUnderComposition] [P.HasOfPostcompProperty P]
    58→  [PreservesLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan F] [HasPullbacks C] [HasPullbacks D]
    59→
    60→lemma closedUnderLimitsOfShape_walkingCospan :
    61→    (P.costructuredArrowObj F (X := X)).IsClosedUnderLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan where
    62→  limitsOfShape_le := by
    63→    rintro Y ⟨pres, hpres⟩
    64→    have h : IsPullback (F.map (pres.π.app .left).left) (F.map (pres.π.app .right).left)
    65→        (F.map (pres.diag.map WalkingCospan.Hom.inl).left)
    66→          (F.map (pres.diag.map WalkingCospan.Hom.inr).left) :=
    67→      IsPullback.of_isLimit_cone <| isLimitOfPreserves
    68→        (CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.toOver F X ⋙ CategoryTheory.Over.forget X) pres.isLimit
    69→    rw [costructuredArrowObj_iff]
    70→    have hw1 : Y.hom = F.map (pres.π.app .left).left ≫ (pres.diag.obj .left).hom := by
    71→      have := (pres.π.app WalkingCospan.left).w
    72→      simp only [Functor.const_obj_obj, Functor.const_obj_map, Category.comp_id] at this
    73→      exact this.symm
    74→    rw [hw1]
    75→    apply P.comp_mem _ _ (P.of_isPullback h.flip ?_) (hpres _)
    76→    have hw2 : F.map (pres.diag.map WalkingCospan.Hom.inr).left ≫
    77→        (pres.diag.obj WalkingCospan.one).hom = (pres.diag.obj WalkingCospan.right).hom := by
    78→      have := (pres.diag.map WalkingCospan.Hom.inr).w
    79→      simp only [Functor.const_obj_obj, Functor.const_obj_map, Category.comp_id] at this
    80→      exact this
    81→    exact P.of_postcomp _ (pres.diag.obj WalkingCospan.one).hom (hpres .one)
    82→      (hw2 ▸ hpres .right)
    83→
    84→noncomputable
    85→instance createsLimitsOfShape_walkingCospan [HasPullbacks C] [HasPullbacks D] :
    86→    CreatesLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (CostructuredArrow.forget P ⊤ F X) :=
    87→  haveI : HasLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (Comma F (Functor.fromPUnit X)) :=
    88→    inferInstanceAs <| HasLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (CostructuredArrow F X)
    89→  have : (commaObj F (Functor.fromPUnit X) P).IsClosedUnderLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan := by
    90→    apply closedUnderLimitsOfShape_walkingCospan
    91→  Comma.forgetCreatesLimitsOfShapeOfClosed P
    92→
    93→instance hasPullbacks : HasPullbacks (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ F X) :=
    94→  haveI : HasLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (Comma F (Functor.fromPUnit X)) :=
    95→    inferInstanceAs <| HasLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (CostructuredArrow F X)
    96→  have : (commaObj F (Functor.fromPUnit X) P).IsClosedUnderLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan := by
    97→    apply closedUnderLimitsOfShape_walkingCospan
    98→  Comma.hasLimitsOfShape_of_closedUnderLimitsOfShape P
    99→
   100→instance : PreservesLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan (CostructuredArrow.toOver P F X) := by
   101→  have : PreservesLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan
   102→      (CostructuredArrow.toOver P F X ⋙ Over.forget P ⊤ X) := by
   103→    show PreservesLimitsOfShape WalkingCospan <|
   104→      CostructuredArrow.forget P ⊤ F X ⋙ CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.toOver F X
   105→    infer_instance
   106→  exact preservesLimitsOfShape_of_reflects_of_preserves _ (Over.forget _ _ X)
   107→
   108→end CostructuredArrow
   109→
   110→end MorphismProperty
   111→
   112→end CategoryTheory
   113→
   114→namespace AlgebraicGeometry
   115→
   116→namespace Scheme
   117→
   118→variable {S : Scheme.{u}}
   119→
   120→section
   121→
   122→@[simps! hom left]
   123→def affineOverMk {P : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u}} {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
   124→    (f : Spec R ⟶ S) (hf : P f) :
   125→    P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S :=
   126→  .mk ⊤ f hf
   127→
   128→instance isCoverDense_toOver_Spec (P : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u}) [P.IsMultiplicative]
   129→    [IsZariskiLocalAtSource P] [P.IsStableUnderBaseChange] [P.HasOfPostcompProperty P] :
   130→    (MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S).IsCoverDense
   131→      (smallGrothendieckTopology P) where
   132→  is_cover U := by
   133→    rw [Scheme.mem_smallGrothendieckTopology]
   134→    let 𝒰 : Cover.{u} (precoverage P) U.left :=
   135→      U.left.affineCover.changeProp
   136→      (fun _ ↦ IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_isOpenImmersion _)
   137→    let _ (i : 𝒰.I₀) : (𝒰.X i).Over S := ⟨𝒰.f i ≫ U.hom⟩
   138→    refine ⟨𝒰, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
   139→    · exact ⟨fun i ↦ inferInstance, fun i ↦ ⟨rfl⟩⟩
   140→    · intro i
   141→      apply P.comp_mem
   142→      exact 𝒰.map_prop i
   143→      exact U.prop
   144→    · rintro X f ⟨i⟩
   145→      simp only [Sieve.coverByImage]
   146→      refine ⟨⟨affineOverMk (𝒰.f i ≫ U.hom) (P.comp_mem _ _ (𝒰.map_prop i) U.prop), ?_, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
   147→      · exact MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.homMk (𝟙 _) ⟨⟩ (Category.id_comp _)
   148→      · dsimp
   149→        exact MorphismProperty.Over.homMk (𝒰.f i) (by simp) trivial
   150→      · ext; dsimp; exact Category.id_comp _
   151→
   152→variable {P : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u}} [IsZariskiLocalAtSource P]
   153→
   154→instance IsZariskiLocalAtSource.isClosedUnderColimitsOfShape_discrete
   155→    {ι : Type*} [Small.{u} ι] {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasColimitsOfShape (Discrete ι) C]
   156→    (L : C ⥤ Scheme.{u}) [PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete ι) L] (X : Scheme.{u}) :
   157→    (P.costructuredArrowObj L (X := X)).IsClosedUnderColimitsOfShape (Discrete ι) := by
   158→  refine CostructuredArrow.isClosedUnderColimitsOfShape ?_ ?_ ?_ _
   159→  · intro D _
   160→    exact Sigma.cocone _
   161→  · intro D
   162→    exact coproductIsCoproduct' _
   163→  · intro D _ X s h
   164→    exact IsZariskiLocalAtSource.sigmaDesc (h ⟨·⟩)
   165→
   166→variable [P.IsStableUnderBaseChange] [P.HasOfPostcompProperty P] [P.IsMultiplicative]
   167→
   168→instance : HasFiniteCoproducts (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S) where
   169→  out n := by
   170→    have : (MorphismProperty.commaObj Scheme.Spec (.fromPUnit S) P).IsClosedUnderColimitsOfShape
   171→        (Discrete (Fin n)) := by
   172→      apply IsZariskiLocalAtSource.isClosedUnderColimitsOfShape_discrete
   173→    apply MorphismProperty.Comma.hasColimitsOfShape_of_closedUnderColimitsOfShape
   174→
   175→/-- `CostructuredArrow.toOver Scheme.Spec S` preserves binary coproducts.
   176→This follows because `proj ⋙ Spec` preserves coproducts (as `proj` creates and `Spec`
   177→preserves), `Over.forget S` reflects colimits (since it creates them), and
   178→`toOver ⋙ Over.forget = proj ⋙ Spec`. -/
   179→noncomputable instance : PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   180→    (CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.toOver Scheme.Spec S) := by
   181→  have : PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   182→      (CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.toOver Scheme.Spec S ⋙
   183→        CategoryTheory.Over.forget S) := by
   184→    show PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair) <|
   185→      CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.proj Scheme.Spec S ⋙ Scheme.Spec
   186→    infer_instance
   187→  exact preservesColimitsOfShape_of_reflects_of_preserves _
   188→    (CategoryTheory.Over.forget S)
   189→
   190→instance : PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   191→    (MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S) := by
   192→  haveI : (MorphismProperty.commaObj Scheme.Spec (.fromPUnit S) P).IsClosedUnderColimitsOfShape
   193→      (Discrete WalkingPair) := by
   194→    apply IsZariskiLocalAtSource.isClosedUnderColimitsOfShape_discrete
   195→  haveI : HasColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   196→      (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S) := inferInstance
   197→  haveI : HasColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   198→      (Comma Scheme.Spec (Functor.fromPUnit S)) :=
   199→    inferInstanceAs <| HasColimitsOfShape _ (CostructuredArrow Scheme.Spec S)
   200→  haveI : CreatesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   201→      (MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.forget P ⊤ Scheme.Spec S) :=
   202→    MorphismProperty.Comma.forgetCreatesColimitsOfShapeOfClosed
   203→      (L := Scheme.Spec) (R := Functor.fromPUnit S) P (Discrete WalkingPair)
   204→  have : PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair)
   205→      (MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S ⋙
   206→        MorphismProperty.Over.forget P ⊤ S) := by
   207→    show PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete WalkingPair) <|
   208→      MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.forget P ⊤ Scheme.Spec S ⋙
   209→        CategoryTheory.CostructuredArrow.toOver Scheme.Spec S
   210→    infer_instance
   211→  exact preservesColimitsOfShape_of_reflects_of_preserves _
   212→    (MorphismProperty.Over.forget P ⊤ S)
   213→
   214→instance : FinitaryExtensive (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S) :=
   215→  CategoryTheory.finitaryExtensive_of_preserves_and_reflects_isomorphism
   216→    (MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S)
   217→
   218→instance : Preregular (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S) := by
   219→  apply Preregular.of_hasPullbacks_of_effectiveEpi_fst
   220→  intro X Y Z f g hg
   221→  let F := MorphismProperty.CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S
   222→  -- The key step: show the underlying scheme map of g is surjective.
   223→  -- This requires: EffectiveEpi g in P.CostructuredArrow → Surjective (F.map g).left
   224→  -- which is non-trivial in general.
   225→  haveI hsur_g : Surjective (F.map g).left := by
   226→    sorry
   227→  -- Show the underlying scheme morphism of pullback.fst in the Over category is surjective.
   228→  haveI : Surjective (pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)).left := by
   229→    show Surjective ((MorphismProperty.Over.forget P ⊤ S ⋙ CategoryTheory.Over.forget S).map
   230→      (pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)))
   231→    rw [← pullbackComparison_comp_fst]
   232→    exact (MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := @Surjective) _ _).mpr (by
   233→      dsimp
   234→      haveI : Surjective ((CategoryTheory.Over.forget S).map
   235→        ((MorphismProperty.Over.forget P ⊤ S).map (F.map g))) := hsur_g
   236→      infer_instance)
   237→  -- pullback.fst in Over category is EffectiveEpi (from Surjective → EffectiveEpi)
   238→  -- NOTE: This step needs EffectiveEpi from Surjective for general P, which may not hold.
   239→  -- For P = @Etale, this is Scheme.Etale.effectiveEpi_of_surjective.
   240→  haveI : EffectiveEpi (pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)) := by
   241→    sorry
   242→  -- F preserves pullbacks and reflects effective epis, so transfer back
   243→  apply F.effectiveEpi_of_map
   244→  rw [show F.map (pullback.fst f g) =
   245→    (PreservesPullback.iso F f g).hom ≫ pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)
   246→    from (PreservesPullback.iso_hom_fst F f g).symm]
   247→  infer_instance
   248→
   249→end

input.content:
# Proetale/Topology/Coherent/Affine.lean

## Status: BLOCKED - Fundamental Infrastructure Gap

## Summary

This file has 2 sorries in a general `Preregular` instance (lines 226 and 241) that are acknowledged in the comments as "non-trivial in general." These sorries are in an instance that attempts to prove `Preregular (P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S)` for any morphism property `P` with certain properties.

## The Two Sorries

### Sorry 1 (line 226): EffectiveEpi → Surjective

**Goal:** Show that if `g : Y ⟶ Z` is an `EffectiveEpi` in `P.CostructuredArrow ⊤ Scheme.Spec S`, then the underlying scheme morphism `(F.map g).left` is surjective, where `F = CostructuredArrow.toOver P Scheme.Spec S`.

**Mathematical Issue:** This requires showing that effective epimorphisms in the costructured arrow category correspond to surjective morphisms on the underlying schemes. This is true for specific morphism properties like `@Etale` (because étale morphisms are universally open, and epis of open morphisms are surjective), but does not hold for arbitrary morphism properties.

### Sorry 2 (line 241): Surjective → EffectiveEpi

**Goal:** Show that if the underlying scheme morphism of `pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)` is surjective, then `pullback.fst (F.map f) (F.map g)` is an `EffectiveEpi` in the Over category.

**Mathematical Issue:** This requires showing that surjective morphisms in the Over category are effective epis. For `P = @Etale`, this follows from `Scheme.Etale.effectiveEpi_of_surjective`, but again does not hold for arbitrary `P`.

## Attempted Approaches

### Attempt 1: Direct proof using `Over.surjective_of_epi_of_isOpenMap`
- **Result:** FAILED
- **Reason:** Cannot synthesize `EffectiveEpi (F.map g)` or `UniversallyOpen (F.map g).left` for general `P`
- **Dead end:** This approach requires `P` to imply `UniversallyOpen`, which is not assumed

### Attempt 2: Specialized instance for `AffineEtale S`
- **Approach:** Create a direct `Preregular (AffineEtale S)` instance that bypasses the general sorries
- **Result:** FAILED
- **Reason:** The functor `AffineEtale.Spec S : AffineEtale S ⥤ S.Etale` does not automatically preserve pullbacks in a way that makes `pullbackComparison` an isomorphism. The nested structure (CostructuredArrow → Over → Scheme) makes it difficult to transfer surjectivity properties.

### Attempt 3: Using pullback preservation
- **Approach:** Try to show `IsIso (pullbackComparison (AffineEtale.Spec S) f g)` and use this to transfer surjectivity
- **Result:** FAILED
- **Reason:** Cannot synthesize `IsIso (pullbackComparison (AffineEtale.Spec S) f g)` - the functor preserves pullbacks but the comparison is not automatically an isomorphism

## Mathematical Analysis

The fundamental issue is that these sorries are asking for properties that depend on the specific morphism property `P`:

1. **For `P = @Etale`:** Both sorries should be fillable because:
   - Étale morphisms are universally open
   - In `S.Etale`, we have `Scheme.Etale.epi_iff_surjective` (line 295-297 in Etale.lean)
   - We have `Scheme.Etale.effectiveEpi_of_surjective` (line 283-287 in Etale.lean)

2. **For general `P`:** These properties do not hold without additional assumptions like:
   - `P` implies `UniversallyOpen`
   - Epis in `P.Over ⊤ S` are surjective
   - Surjective morphisms in `P.Over ⊤ S` are effective epis

## Recommendation

**Accept these sorries as fundamental infrastructure gaps.** The comments in the code explicitly acknowledge these are "non-trivial in general" and note that "For P = @Etale, this is Scheme.Etale.effectiveEpi_of_surjective."

The actual use case (`AffineEtale S` at line 320) works because it inherits the instance via `inferInstanceAs`, and the sorries don't prevent the file from compiling. The `Precoherent` instance (line 323) also works.

## Alternative: Restrict to Étale

If we wanted to fill these sorries, we would need to either:
1. Add type class assumptions to the general instance that ensure the required properties
2. Make the instance specific to `P = @Etale` only
3. Prove the missing infrastructure lemmas about the relationship between effective epis in costructured arrow categories and surjectivity

Option 3 would require significant categorical infrastructure work (50-100+ lines) and may be better suited for a Mathlib PR.

## Next Steps

- Mark this file as having fundamental infrastructure gaps that should be accepted
- The file compiles and the instances work despite the sorries
- If upstream wants these filled, they should be addressed as part of a broader Mathlib contribution about costructured arrow categories and morphism properties

1→# Archon Project
     2→
     3→You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
     4→
     5→## Priority Rule
     6→
     7→If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
     8→- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
     9→- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
    10→- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
    11→
    12→When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
    13→
    14→## Skills
    15→- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
    16→
    17→## Tools
    18→- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
    19→- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
    20→
    21→## Key Files & Permissions
    22→
    23→All state files are in `.archon/`:
    24→
    25→| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Review Agent | User |
    26→|------|-----------|-------------|-------------|------|
    27→| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    28→| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | write |
    29→| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    30→| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    31→| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | read only | read |
    32→| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    33→| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    34→| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only) | do not edit | write (via comments) |
    35→
    36→## User Interaction
    37→
    38→Users provide hints in two places:
    39→
    40→- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
    41→- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
    42→
    43→## Agent Roles
    44→
    45→### Plan Agent
    46→- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
    47→- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
    48→- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
    49→- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
    50→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
    51→
    52→### Prover Agent
    53→- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
    54→- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
    55→  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
    56→  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
    57→  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
    58→- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
    59→- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
    60→- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
    61→
    62→### Review Agent
    63→- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
    64→- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
    65→- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
    66→- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
    67→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
    68→

1→# Prover — Prover Stage
     2→
     3→You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
     4→
     5→## Workflow
     6→
     7→1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
     8→2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
     9→3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
    10→4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
    11→5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
    12→6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
    13→7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
    14→
    15→**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
    16→
    17→## Avoid Early Termination
    18→
    19→- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
    20→- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
    21→- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
    22→- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
    23→- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
    24→- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
    25→
    26→## Task Completion Criteria
    27→
    28→Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
    29→1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
    30→2. Zero axioms introduced
    31→3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
    32→
    33→If you encounter obstacles:
    34→- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
    35→- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
    36→- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
    37→- Try alternative proof strategies
    38→- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
    39→- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
    40→
    41→### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
    42→
    43→Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
    44→
    45→1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
    46→2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
    47→3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
    48→   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
    49→   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
    50→   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
    51→
    52→## Proof Style
    53→
    54→- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
    55→- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
    56→- Do not add unrelated declarations
    57→- **Initial definitions and final theorem/lemma statements are frozen** — do not modify them. If a statement appears wrong, keep the file compilable (use scoped `sorry`), explain why in `task_pending.md`, and let the plan agent decide.
    58→- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
    59→- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
    60→
    61→## Search Protocol
    62→
    63→Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
    64→
    65→1. `lean_local_search` first
    66→2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
    67→3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
    68→4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
    69→
    70→## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
    71→
    72→Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
    73→- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
    74→- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
    75→
    76→## Logging
    77→
    78→Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
    79→
    80→**Format:**
    81→
    82→```markdown
    83→# Algebra/WLocal.lean
    84→
    85→## wLocal_iff (line 45)
    86→### Attempt 1
    87→- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
    88→- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
    89→- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
    90→
    91→### Attempt 2
    92→- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
    93→- **Result:** RESOLVED
    94→- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
    95→
    96→## helper_bijective (line 78)
    97→### Attempt 1
    98→- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
    99→- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
   100→- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
   101→- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
   102→```
   103→
   104→**Rules:**
   105→1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
   106→2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
   107→3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
   108→4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
   109→
   110→**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
   111→
   112→## Summary Pipeline
   113→
   114→1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
   115→2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
   116→3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
   117→4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
   118→5. Do not modify initial definitions or final theorem/lemma statements. Only fill in proof bodies and add helper lemmas. Intermediate helpers you introduced may be corrected.
   119→6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
   120→7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
   121→8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
   122→
   123→## End-of-session handoff
   124→
   125→Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
   126→
   127→1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
   128→   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
   129→   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
   130→   - Concrete next step for the next session
   131→   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
   132→2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
   133→

     6→import Proetale.Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Limits
     7→import Proetale.Topology.Comparison.Etale
     8→import Proetale.Topology.Coherent.Affine
     9→import Proetale.Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Continuous
    10→import Proetale.Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.StructuredArrow.Basic
    11→import Proetale.Pro.PresheafColimit
    12→
    13→universe w u
    14→
    15→open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
    16→
    17→namespace CategoryTheory
    18→
    19→variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category D]
    20→  (J : GrothendieckTopology C) (K : GrothendieckTopology D)
    21→  (L : C ⥤ D) (R : D ⥤ C)
    22→  {A : Type*} [Category A]
    23→
    24→namespace MorphismProperty
    25→
    26→variable {J : Type*} [Category* J] {C : Type*} [Category* C]
    27→variable {P Q : MorphismProperty C} [Q.IsMultiplicative]
    28→
    29→@[simps!]
    30→def Over.lift' (D : J ⥤ C) {S : C} (s : D ⟶ (Functor.const J).obj S)
    31→    (hs : ∀ j, P (s.app j)) (hD : ∀ {i j} (f : i ⟶ j), Q (D.map f)) :
    32→    J ⥤ P.Over Q S :=
    33→  Over.lift (CategoryTheory.Over.lift D s) hs hD
    34→
    35→@[simps]
    36→def Over.iteratedLift {S : C} (D : J ⥤ P.Over Q S)
    37→    {X : P.Over Q S}
    38→    (s : D ⟶ (Functor.const J).obj X) (hs : ∀ j, P (s.app j).left)
    39→    (hD : ∀ {i j} (f : i ⟶ j), Q (D.map f).left := by cat_disch) :
    40→    J ⥤ P.Over Q X.left where
    41→  obj j := Over.mk _ (s.app j).left (hs j)
    42→  map {i j} f := Over.homMk (D.map f).left
    43→    (by simpa [-NatTrans.naturality] using congr($(s.naturality f).left)) (hD f)
    44→
    45→end MorphismProperty
    46→
    47→end CategoryTheory
    48→
    49→namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
    50→
    51→section
    52→
    53→variable {S T : Scheme.{u}} (f : S ⟶ T)
    54→  (P : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u}) [P.IsMultiplicative] [P.RespectsIso]
    55→  [P.IsStableUnderBaseChange]
    56→variable (A : Type*) [Category* A]
    57→
    58→instance :
    59→    (Over.pullback P ⊤ f).PreservesOneHypercovers
    60→      (T.smallGrothendieckTopology P)
    61→      (S.smallGrothendieckTopology P) := by
    62→  intro X E
    63→  constructor
    64→  · -- mem₀: Need (E.map (Over.pullback P ⊤ f)).sieve₀ ∈ S.smallGrothendieckTopology P.
    65→    -- Since smallGrothendieckTopology P = (forget P ⊤ S).inducedTopology (overGrothendieckTopology P S),
    66→    -- this reduces to showing the pushforward of the image sieve through Over.forget is in
    67→    -- S.overGrothendieckTopology P. The original hypercover E gives a P-cover of X.left;
    68→    -- base change along pullback.fst X.hom f gives a P-cover of pullback X.hom f
    69→    -- (using P.IsStableUnderBaseChange). Missing: formal infrastructure for relating
    70→    -- sieve operations across the induced topology and the pullback functor.
    71→    rw [Functor.mem_inducedTopology_sieves_iff]
    72→    sorry
    73→  · -- mem₁: Similar argument for the fiber product sieves.
    74→    intro i₁ i₂ W p₁ p₂ hw
    75→    rw [Functor.mem_inducedTopology_sieves_iff]
    76→    sorry
    77→
    78→noncomputable
    79→abbrev smallPushforward :
    80→    Sheaf (S.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A ⥤ Sheaf (T.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A :=
    81→  (Over.pullback P ⊤ f).sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _
    82→
    83→-- Requires IsContinuous (from PreservesOneHypercovers above) + existence of left adjoint
    84→-- (HasWeakSheafify or HasLeftKanExtension for the small site categories).
    85→instance :
    86→    ((Over.pullback P ⊤ f).sheafPushforwardContinuous A (smallGrothendieckTopology P)
    87→      (smallGrothendieckTopology P)).IsRightAdjoint :=
    88→  sorry
    89→
    90→noncomputable
    91→abbrev smallPullback :
    92→    Sheaf (T.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A ⥤ Sheaf (S.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A :=
    93→  (Over.pullback P ⊤ f).sheafPullback _ _ _
    94→
    95→noncomputable
    96→def smallPullbackPushforwardAdj :
    97→    smallPullback f P A ⊣ smallPushforward f P A :=
    98→  (Over.pullback P ⊤ f).sheafAdjunctionContinuous A _ _
    99→
   100→-- IsContinuous for Over.map ⊤ hf between smallGrothendieckTopology.
   101→-- Approach 1 (CoverPreserving + CompatiblePreserving): CoverPreserving follows from the
   102→-- commutative diagram Over.map ⊤ hf ⋙ Over.forget P ⊤ T = Over.forget P ⊤ S ⋙ Over.map f
   103→-- and over_map_coverPreserving. CompatiblePreserving is blocked: needs RepresentablyFlat
   104→-- (hard to establish) or IsCoverDense+IsLocallyFaithful (Over.map is not cover dense).
   105→-- Approach 2 (PreservesOneHypercovers): mem₀ works (same underlying cover). mem₁ is blocked:
   106→-- needs to construct W' : P.Over ⊤ S from W : P.Over ⊤ T (requires HasOfPostcompProperty P).
   107→-- Approach 3 (direct): use IsContinuous of Over.map f for over topologies and transfer through
   108→-- the induced topology structure. Missing formal infrastructure for this transfer.
   109→instance (hf : P f) :
   110→    (Over.map ⊤ hf).IsContinuous (smallGrothendieckTopology P) (smallGrothendieckTopology P) := by
   111→  sorry
   112→
   113→def smallSheafRestrict (hf : P f) :
   114→    Sheaf (T.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A ⥤ Sheaf (S.smallGrothendieckTopology P) A :=
   115→  (Over.map ⊤ hf).sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _
   116→
   117→noncomputable def smallSheafRestrictAdj (hf : P f) :
   118→    smallSheafRestrict f P A hf ⊣ smallPushforward f P A :=

   122→naturally isomorphic to the restriction functor. -/

   124→    smallPullback f P A ≅ smallSheafRestrict f P A hf :=

   126→    (Iso.refl _)

   132→/-- An object of the pro-étale site of `S` is pro-affine, if it can be written
   133→as `limᵢ (Spec Aᵢ)` where `Spec Aᵢ` is an étale `S`-scheme. -/
   134→def ProEt.proAffine (S : Scheme.{u}) : ObjectProperty S.ProEt :=
   135→  fun X ↦ ∃ (J : Type u) (_ : SmallCategory J) (_ : IsCofiltered J),
   136→    Nonempty (RelativeLimitPresentation J (AffineEtale.Spec S ⋙ S.toProEtale) X)
   137→

   139→object can be written as a cofiltered limit of affine étale schemes over `S`. -/
   140→def AffineProEt (S : Scheme.{u}) : Type (u + 1) :=

   154→
   155→instance : (toProEt S).Full := inferInstanceAs <| (ProEt.proAffine S).ι.Full

   179→noncomputable
   180→def sheafPushforward :

   188→it is a Zariksi sheaf and satifies the sheaf condition for single surjective morphisms. -/
   189→lemma isSheaf {F : (AffineProEt S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type*}
   190→    (h₁ : Presheaf.IsSheaf (zariskiTopology S) F)
